After the match, United maintained their six-point gap from Chelsea, as Cristiano continued his outstanding performances in the next months, as Mendes and the club agreed on extending Cristiano's contract until 2012, with a weekly wage that amounted to £119,000. In the Champions League, Cristiano and United were performing on a high level. As they scored seven goals against Roma in the second leg of the quarter-finals, Cristiano managed to score a brace and assist another. It was his first two goals in the Champions League. United then faced Milan in the semi-finals, and although Ronaldo managed to score a goal as the team won by three goals to two in the first leg, he struggled to put a stamp on the match. And when he played at the San Siro, Cristiano struggled again as he started on the left and was under tight man marking from Gennaro Gattuso as he failed to influence the match. As United lost by three goals to nil and were out of the competition. In the Premier League and after Chelsea drew with Arsenal, Manchester United managed to seal their first Premier League title in four years. The clash with Mourinho and Chelsea was repeated in the FA Cup final. Ronaldo started the match on the left side but the match was timid and mostly eventless and saw a rare few chances. The match was decided by Drogba's winner in extra time. Cristiano produced by far his best ever season. In his first three seasons with United, he played a total of 137 matches and scored 27 goals. In this season only, he managed to score 23 goals in 54 matches and with 24 assists. He was named Player of the Year and Young Player of the Year by the Professional Footballers Association in England. It's a feat not achieved since 1977 when Andy Gray won both awards. Cristiano managed to win everybody over, the media, the fans and even the opposing players that voted for him. There was no doubt that Cristiano was now one of the best in Europe. The new season was expected to be a special one for Ronaldo, without any international duties in the summer and with great reinforcements for United in the transfer window. But the season didn't start in the best possible way, as United started their defence of the title with two draws and a defeat, while Cristiano was sent off in the second league match against Portsmouth. Ferguson's assistant, the Dutchman René Molensteen, took advantage of Cristiano's three-match ban to introduce some new ideas for the Portuguese star and to analyse Cristiano's previous matches directly with him. Rennie first asked Ronaldo to set a target for the amount of goals he thinks he could score in the new season. Cristiano said that he can score between about 30 to 35 goals, whereas Rennie said that he can get up to 40 goals. Rennie then proceeded to show Cristiano the tactics of strikers and showed him footage of Dennis Law, George Best, Ruud van Nistelrooy, and Solskjaer. George Best was similar to Cristiano in terms of scoring outstanding goals, but the rest were more clinical strikers. Scoring with one touch, scoring inside the box, headers, and of course, volleys. Cristiano was then assigned solo sessions to work on his visualization of goals, movements inside the box, choosing the best spots to score, runs and other technical aspects. When Cristiano came back from his three-match ban, he managed to surpass his goal tally from last season in January of the current season. Cristiano started scoring all kinds of goals, and he started acquiring a nose for goal. In that season, we saw several headers. When he scored his first header of the season against Wigan, it came as a surprise for Cristiano. In the same month, he scored another header, but this time with a better finish. But Cristiano was still surprised with himself. In the following month, Cristiano jumped to score a fantastic header against Blackburn. And this time, it was not a surprise. Headers were now a big part of Cristiano's arsenal of attributes. A match against Fulham in December showed his wide range of goals. The first came through a beautiful and instinctive volley, and the second goal came through a header any striker would be proud of. In another match against Everton, we saw him cut from the right to the left and shoot with his weak foot from range. Ronaldo's huge development in the year 2007 earned him the second position in the Ballon d'Or rankings behind Kaká and just ahead of Messi. He was now very close to the top, but he still needed to win more titles, a title like the Champions League. And after Cristiano seeded the tie against Lyon in the round of 16, United faced Roma again. The first leg was pretty tough on United, and in the first half, Scholes lifted the ball up high as Ronaldo soared from far away to score a beautiful and important header. As United passed this tough test and got to another semi-final in the Champions League. United then faced Barcelona, and it was the first official match between Cristiano and Messi. Carlos Queiroz suggested the change in tactics before the match for Cristiano, as the Portuguese started the tie as a striker, as Queiroz thought that Ronaldo would face issues in covering the right side of Barcelona as they used Park Ji-sung to cover that right side and to specifically mark Messi. Ronaldo didn't like that change as he struggled for possession on the ball in the Camp Nou and missed an early and crucial penalty. The tie was difficult on both sides and was eventually decided by a long-range effort from Scholes And the return leg as Manchester United got to their first Champions League final since 1999. In the league, Cristiano continued scoring goals as the race for the league title heated up and got all the way to the final day. United travelled to face Wigan and Ronaldo got the chance to erase the memories of Barcelona as the Portuguese stepped up to take a crucial penalty in the title race. United won by two goals, whereas Chelsea drew with Bolton as Cristiano lifted a new title. A rematch of the title race happened in the champions league final ronaldo played on the left side and was given freedom to roam and take advantage of his speed to take on the blues defense which used michael essien as a right back the match was expected to be difficult in general as there was tight marking on cristiano and sometimes he was marked by three players whom were essienien, Balak, and Jokorn. In the past, Cristiano would have struggled, but with his development in the last few seasons, he managed to overcome the marking with his positive runs without the ball into the box. And with those movements, he managed to score a great header. After Lampard scored the equalizer, the match eventually went to a penalty shootout. And after two successful penalties for both sides, Cristiano stepped up to shoot. Fortunately for Ronaldo, Terry slipped on the crucial fifth penalty. Then Van der Sar managed to save an ilkish shot to help United lift the trophy, and for Cristiano to lift the title that may hand him the Ballon d'Or. A double for Cristiano and another outstanding season for the Portuguese star as he won the European Golden Boot Award after scoring 31 goals in the league and 42 goals overall. So you can't blame the fans for being excited for another season of Ronaldo. Carlos Queiroz mentioned to Ferguson in the past that if you manage to get five years out of Cristiano, then you've struck gold. There is no precedent for a Portuguese teenager to go to another country and stay for five years. There were some hints that pointed to Cristiano leaving, as he mentioned before the Champions League final, that he dreams of playing in Spain. That's why it was expected that Ronaldo's sixth season with United will be a complicated one for management. But before all that, there was the Euros in the summer. Portugal came to the Euros and it was obvious that Cristiano was now the main man of the team as he had joined captaincy along with his teammate Nuno Gomez. Portugal ended the group stages as group leaders, but Cristiano and his teammates faced the Germans in the next round. Bastian Schweinsteiger was the star of the match, while Cristiano's efforts were not enough as Portugal crashed out of the tournament. With all the rumors surrounding Ronaldo, Ferguson traveled to Portugal to sit with Cristiano in Carlos Queiroz's house. Long story short, Ferguson refused to sell Ronaldo to Real Madrid, especially after how their president, Ramon Calderon, dealt with the matter. For Sir Alex, it was a matter of maintaining his authority in the global stage. And Ferguson mentioned in his autobiography that if Ronaldo stayed for one more year and gave it his all, he promised him that if any club came with a record offer, he'll agree to sell. Cristiano agreed, and the matter was closed. Unfortunately for Cristiano and United, it was his weakest season when compared to his last two seasons at that time, as he had a niggling injury since March of 2008. The injury eventually got worse after the match against Germany in the Euros, and it was agreed that he needed surgery on his ankle and was out of action for six whole weeks. Ronaldo came back in September and his goal was clear, ending the season in the best possible way to seal the Ballon d'Or. That's why he came back to the line-up and got back to scoring goals immediately. But with each goal he scored, especially in the beginning, it came with a subdued celebration, and he started to distance himself from his teammates. As Gary Neville said, he was very professional that whole season, but he was counting down the months until he could leave. Despite all that, Cristiano eventually got back to his best of forms and scored his 100th goal for United in November. Cristiano few weeks, the winner of the Ballon d'Or was announced in December, and there was no doubt that Cristiano was the most deserving of this award, as he won it by a big margin ahead of Lionel Messi in second and Fernando Torres in third. As Cristiano said, this trophy is so important that I want to win it again. I'll wake up every day and say to myself, I have to keep improving. Cristiano then travelled to Japan to help United win another trophy, which was the Club World Cup. He managed to score a goal in a hectic semi-final and handed an assist in the final. He came back to the Premier League and the Champions League to score some very crucial goals. A goal to seal the tie against Inter in the round of 16, an outrageous long-range effort to seal the tie against Porto in the quarter-finals, and two goals at the Emirates Stadium against Arsenal in the semi-finals. He then proceeded to score a brace against both Villa and Spurs as United moved away in the title race and sealed the title in the last few weeks. Cristiano won the League, the League Cup and the Club World Cup. What was left was the second Champions League final in a row, as the tie against Barcelona and Messi was repeated again. Ronaldo made life hard for Victor Valdez in the opening minutes. Then Pep Guardiola changed the match, when he pushed Eto'o to the right and Messi as a full striker, as Barcelona took over possession of the ball and managed to score two goals and eventually killed the the match. وكوتي بايس من شديد لسيد أوروبا FC بارسيلونا كل الوثائق تدخلت إلى كريستيانو في لعبته الأخيرة لأمريكا خاصة بعد أن كالديرون قد تسقط وقام برئيس لتحضير رئيسية ريال مدريد أزال ميريغي أتيت بإعطاء رقم وكما أمر فرغسون أدعى كريستيانو أن يترك مجال جديد في قصة كريستيانو ترجمة نانسي قنقر Madrid as El Merengue came with a record offer and as Ferguson promised, he let Cristiano leave. A new page in Cristiano's story as the Madrid fans filled the Santiago Bernabeu, excited to see the man brought to end Barcelona's reign of dominance and get Madrid back to being European champions. And for Cristiano, he finally got what he wanted after years of hard work and his strive to be the best. So what does the future hold for the Portuguese star? ¡Aquí viene Ronaldo!

Mendes planned to pick up three Sporting Academy players, whom were Quaresma, Viana, and Ronaldo, all of whom were represented by Jose Vega. Mendes sat with these players and promised them that he'll find big clubs that are interested in their talents, and that will help them earn no less than a million euros in a flash. But actually it was enough to attract the attention of several European clubs, and Sporting hadn't won a single title that season, so the manager Bologna was fired. At the end of the season, Mendes warned Sporting that if Cristiano was not a starter next year, there was a big chance he would leave for another club. And Jorge Mendes was very active that year, as he lined up several offers for Cristiano, and the Portuguese teenager said after the Toulon tournament that he was pleased to receive the attention of many important clubs and he dreams of playing in Spain or England. Now let's look at who were the real candidates to sign Cristiano in the summer of 2003. Luis Colari gave Cristiano some national team caps before the Euros, which were held in Portugal that summer, and Cristiano successfully joined Colari's final squad for the summer tournament and became the sixth youngest player in the competition's history. He arrived at the training camp full of confidence, as Colari said that he's a good kid, but someone must have told him he's the best in the world, and if he thinks he is, it will be very hard to work with him. In the group stages, Cristiano was gaining more minutes as the tournament progressed. In the opener against Greece, Cristiano entered in the second half and gave away a penalty, and then scored a goal, but Portugal eventually lost. And following an assist in the win against Russia, Cristiano started the crucial match against Spain, as Portugal won, and Cristiano was named man of the match. But the match ended just like the opener of the tournament, as Greece won against all odds. Cristiano missed a huge chance, and was very upset after the loss. Cristiano came back to United a different person, as the assistant Mike Phelan had mentioned he came back a man his body shape was different his facial features were different as well and we just thought that's an athletic physique for the British game physically he was different but his performances remained inconsistent and that started to wane on his partner on the right Gary Neville who questioned the Portuguese's defensive duties.
